Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
1984-09-21
1. Coronary artery bypass graft times 2; left internal
    mammary artery to left anterior descending artery and
    saphenous vein graft to diagonal artery.
2. Closure of ostium secundum atrial septal defect.
3. Endoscopic harvesting of the long saphenous vein.

History of Present Illness:
This 64-year-old patient with recent
stress test which was positive was admitted for elective
coronary artery bypass graft.  The left ventricular function
is well-preserved.  Intraoperative transesophageal
echocardiogram showed good-sized ostial secundum septal
defect.  The plan was to proceed with coronary artery bypass
grafting and closure of the atrial septal defect.

Pertinent Results:
PRE-CPB: 1. The left atrium and right atrium are normal in
cavity size. No thrombus is seen in the left atrial appendage.
2. A small secundum atrial septal defect is present.
3. Overall left ventricular systolic function is mildly
depressed (LVEF= 45 %).
4. Right ventricular chamber size and free wall motion are
normal.
5. The ascending, transverse and descending thoracic aorta are
normal in diameter and free of atherosclerotic plaque. There are
simple atheroma in the descending thoracic aorta.
6. There are three aortic valve leaflets. The aortic valve
leaflets (3) are mildly thickened. There is no aortic valve
stenosis. Trace aortic regurgitation is seen.
7. Trivial mitral regurgitation is seen.
Dr. Perry was notified in person of the results.

POST-CPB: On infusion of phenylephrine. AV pacing, then sinus
rhythm. Preserved biventricular systolic function. There is
trivial flow across the repaired asd. The aortic contour is
normal post decannulation.

Brief Hospital Course:
The patient was brought to the operating room on 03-16 where the
patient underwent:
1. Coronary artery bypass graft times 2; left internal
    mammary artery to left anterior descending artery and
    saphenous vein graft to diagonal artery.
2. Closure of ostium secundum atrial septal defect.
3. Endoscopic harvesting of the long saphenous vein

Overall the patient tolerated the procedure well and
post-operatively was transferred to the CVICU in stable
condition for recovery and invasive monitoring.
POD 1 found the patient extubated, alert and oriented and
breathing comfortably.  The patient was neurologically intact
and hemodynamically stable, weaned from inotropic and
vasopressor support.  Beta blocker was initiated and the patient
was gently diuresed toward the preoperative weight.

The patient was transferred to the telemetry floor for further
recovery.  Chest tubes and pacing wires were discontinued
without complication.  The patient was evaluated by the physical
therapy service for assistance with strength and mobility.  She
was very weak and extremely slow to progress.

By the time of discharge on POD 5 the patient was ambulating
freely, the wound was healing and pain was controlled with oral
analgesics.  The patient was discharged to The Midmichigan Medical Center-gratiot Rehab in
good condition with appropriate follow up instructions.

Discharge Instructions:
Please shower daily including washing incisions gently with mild
soap, no baths or swimming, and look at your incisions
Please NO lotions, cream, powder, or ointments to incisions
Each morning you should weigh yourself and then in the evening
take your temperature, these should be written down on the chart
No driving for approximately one month and while taking
narcotics, will be discussed at follow up appointment with
surgeon when you will be able to drive
No lifting more than 10 pounds for 10 weeks
Please call with any questions or concerns 133-998-3608
**Please call cardiac surgery office with any questions or
concerns 133-998-3608. Answering service will contact on call
person during off hours**
Females: Please wear bra to reduce pulling on incision, avoid
rubbing on lower edge
